mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 7.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth expectations, boosting consumer spending and driving immediate equity upside.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 7.0 percent over a 5-year test window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and housing starts signals broad-based economic acceleration, compressing risk premiums and generating positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in at least 60% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of accelerating real GDP growth paired with rising core inflation, equity markets price in higher terminal rates, causing forward quarterly returns to turn negative.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain positive in over 65% of observations where real GDP growth exceeds 2.5% and CPI rises above 2.0% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding corporate capacity and output, which directly supports earnings revisions and drives positive short-term equity performance.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.02 across all rolling 12-month windows, or forward 5-day returns show no statistical difference between rising and falling INDPRO periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 0.25 percent, the Federal Reserve signals a normalization phase that increases discount rates and compresses equity multiples over the following month.
failure_condition: Equity markets deliver positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of periods where FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.25 percent, contradicting the discount rate compression thesis.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in nonfarm payrolls growth and retail sales volume confirms durable consumer demand, which sustains earnings momentum and lifts equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to generate positive 60-day forward returns in at least 55% of test periods, or the cross-product term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a housing-led recovery regime, surging housing starts act as a leading indicator for construction and consumer wealth effects, producing immediate positive equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months where HOUST exceeds 1000 units, invalidating the housing wealth effect transmission channel.
